title: Extreme floods damaged more than half of Sindh’s cropland
keyFactLabel: Of cropland in Sindh, Pakistan was damaged due to extreme floods

scenes.text: This is the Sindh province in southeastern Pakistan.

One-third of the province is agricultural land, and is central to Pakistan's food supply. The region produces staple cereals such as wheat and rice, industrial crops including cotton and sugarcane, as well as fruit (mango and guava) and horticultural crops (chili, tomato and sunflower).
In August 2021, much of the Sindh area was green. Positive values in NDVI reflect normal vegetation conditions, while negative values correspond to flood-induced damage and reduced greenness.

During the floods in August 2022, an analysis projected severe production losses, including 88 percent (3.1 million bales) of cotton, 80 percent (1.8 million tons) of rice, and 61 percent (10.5 million tons) of sugarcane.[reference: naturesr23]

As a result, [emphasis: the floods affected an estimated 33 million people], or roughly 13 percent of Pakistan’s population[reference: naturesr23] and approximately 57 percent of Sindh’s cropland (large negative NDVI difference compared to 2021), that is 2.8 million out of 4.9 million hectares.
